memories.memory_text: I remember the blood falling on the snow—three drops like cannon fire—and how the Queen touched her stomach, quiet and still, after pricking her finger on the rose.
grounded_facts: The Queen pricked her finger on a rose blooming in winter.
Three drops of blood fell onto the snow with dramatic emphasis.
The Queen touched her stomach after the blood fell.
The image of blood on snow inspired a wish for a child with specific traits.

memories.memory_text: I remember the winter after the queen died—it was the worst in years, and the garden lost its rose. The king walked in the snow with his arm around the girl, both of them broken, and I knew then that the kingdom had begun to fall.
grounded_facts: Eric's voiceover narrates that the winter following Snow White's mother's death was the harshest in memory.
The cloister garden, once blooming with a red rose, is shown covered in snow and devoid of the flower.
King Magnus is seen walking with Snow White in the snowy garden, appearing heartbroken.
Eric observes the emotional state of the king and princess, linking it to the kingdom's decline.

memories.memory_text: I threw my hatchet and missed on purpose, letting Broch think he had me—then he swung and buried his axe in the oak, just like I knew he would.
grounded_facts: Eric throws a hatchet that misses and sticks in a tree. Broch attacks Eric with a battle axe. Eric ducks at the last moment
causing Broch's axe to become stuck in a tree. Eric smiles darkly
indicating he anticipated this outcome. Eric uses the handle of his second hatchet to knock Broch out.

memories.memory_text: After I knocked Broch out, I searched his pockets, then looked at the girl—Rose—and when I lifted her chin, she smiled back like she wanted more; so I grabbed her and threw her over my shoulder.
grounded_facts: Eric searches Broch's pockets after knocking him out. Eric approaches Rose and lifts her chin. Rose smiles invitingly in response. Eric picks Rose up and tosses her over his shoulder. Rose does not resist or protest.

memories.memory_text: I remember the merchant finally saying ten gold pieces, and how I paused not because I doubted, but because I was making sure he meant it—then I turned and took the deal.
grounded_facts: Eric was haggling with a slave merchant over the sale of Rose.
The merchant initially offered two, then four, and finally ten gold pieces.
Eric hesitated before accepting the final offer, not out of guilt but to confirm the price.
Eric smiled and accepted the offer when the merchant agreed to pay ten gold pieces.

memories.memory_text: I grabbed her by the throat when she mentioned my wife—something in her expression, like she was enjoying it, made me let go fast.
grounded_facts: Eric grabbed Ravenna by the throat in response to her mentioning his wife.
Ravenna did not resist and appeared to enjoy the contact, which disturbed Eric.
Eric released his grip upon noticing her reaction.

memories.memory_text: She said she could bring Sara back—a life for a life—and for a second, I wanted to believe her more than anything.
grounded_facts: Ravenna claimed she had the power to bring Eric's wife back to life in exchange for retrieving the fugitive.
Eric expressed disbelief but showed visible desire to believe her.
The offer was framed as 'a life for a life.'

memories.memory_text: I stepped carefully on the grassy stones, feeling each one give way beneath my boot—told the others to do exactly as I did, no room for mistakes in that mire.
grounded_facts: Eric instructed the group to follow his exact movements while crossing the bog.
He stepped on temporary grassy stones that sank under his weight.
He demonstrated the path first, ensuring others replicated his actions.

memories.memory_text: I saw the faint footprints in the dew, bent down to confirm—they were fresh. Started moving faster then, knowing we were no longer just traveling, but chasing.
grounded_facts: Eric spotted faint footprints in the dew after crossing the bog.
He stopped to examine them closely, indicating recognition of their significance.
His pace increased afterward, shifting the group's mission toward pursuit.

memories.memory_text: I felt it before I saw it—the wrongness in the air—so I stopped and held up my hand, then moved fast ahead of the others. They hesitated, but I couldn’t wait.
grounded_facts: Eric stops and holds up a hand, sensing something.
Eric darts ahead, leaving the group behind.
The group follows reluctantly after a delay.

memories.memory_text: I asked her why the Queen wants her dead, and when she said the Queen wants them all dead, I pressed—what makes you so damn valuable? That’s when I started seeing there’s more to this than just another hunt.
grounded_facts: Eric asked Snow White why the Queen wants her dead.
Snow White responded that the Queen wants them all dead.
Eric questioned what makes Snow White specifically valuable.

memories.memory_text: She said I'd be rewarded if I took her to the Duke’s Castle, and that I’d be dead if I returned without her. I walked away, but she followed. I didn’t answer, but I stopped.
grounded_facts: Snow White claimed Eric would be rewarded if he delivered her to the Duke’s Castle.
Snow White claimed Eric would be dead if he returned without her.
Eric initially walked away from Snow White after their encounter.
Snow White followed Eric after he began to leave.
Eric stopped walking away after Snow White made her appeal.

memories.memory_text: I heard my name and saw Sara behind the rocks. I called out to her, wondering if I was dreaming, and started walking toward her.
grounded_facts: Eric hears a voice calling his name and sees an apparition of Sara.
Eric questions whether he is dreaming.
Eric begins to walk toward the apparition before being interrupted.

memories.memory_text: I smelled the dropping, worked it between my fingers, and knew right away—fumee. When I saw the sign, I told Snow White to stay put and moved ahead to track it, even though my head was still ringing from what I'd seen.
grounded_facts: Eric examines a brown pellet on the ground, identifies it as a fumee dropping.
He warns Snow White to stay behind and moves into the trees to track the threat.
He uses his senses—smell and touch—to confirm the danger before taking action.

memories.memory_text: I turned and saw the figures rising from the marsh banks, arrows drawn, and pulled my axe—stepped in front of Snow White before she could say anything. I didn’t know if they were friend or foe, only that they blocked every way out.
grounded_facts: Eric sees ghostly backlit figures with bows and arrows emerging on the banks of the marsh.
Eric draws his axe and steps in front of Snow White to protect her.
Eric is uncertain about the identity and intentions of the figures, and looks for escape routes.

memories.memory_text: I walked away from the hut, knowing she was better off without me. When Anna confronted me, I told her the truth—everything I’ve ever cared about got destroyed, and I won’t let it happen to Snow White.
grounded_facts: Eric leaves the hut at night after seeing Snow White inside.
Anna confronts Eric outside the village, questioning his decision to leave.
Eric states that he is 'the last thing she needs' and believes he brings bad luck.
Eric references past loss and destruction tied to his presence.

memories.memory_text: I was moving away from the village when I saw the sky light up behind me—burning arrows raining down. I turned and ran back without thinking.
grounded_facts: Eric was leaving the village when he noticed the sky illuminated by fire.
He observed burning arrows falling on the village.
He immediately changed direction and rushed back toward the village.

memories.memory_text: I arrived and saw the village already burning—smoke thick in the air, flames eating the huts, people screaming. The first mercenary never saw me coming.
grounded_facts: Eric arrives at a village that is on fire
He sees the destruction and chaos of the burning village
He pulls a mercenary from his horse and kills him with the mercenary's own sword

memories.memory_text: After dropping the first one, I didn't stop—two more came at me, swinging wild. I took them down fast, one after the other, moving through the smoke and blood.
grounded_facts: Eric engages in combat with two additional mercenaries
He kills both in succession while advancing through the village
The fighting occurs in the context of ongoing chaos and destruction

memories.memory_text: I grabbed Snow White and pulled her away from the flames—she was fighting me, but I couldn't let her go back. Anna shouted for her to go, and I knew we had no time.
grounded_facts: Eric physically intervened to remove Snow White from danger.
Snow White resisted by flailing with a knife.
Anna shouted encouragement for Snow White to leave with Eric.
The scene took place at night amid flames in Fenland Village.

memories.scene_title: 75、EXT. MARSHES OUTSIDE FENLAND VILLAGE/ LAKE - NIGHT.
memories.memory_text: I saw Broch step out of the dark, axe in hand, grinning like this was some kind of game—so I dropped Snow White’s hand, moved fast, and put both hatchets in his ribs before he could swing again.
grounded_facts: Eric sees Broch appear and unstrap his axe.
Eric lets go of Snow White and unstraps his hatchets.
Eric kills Broch by driving both hatchets into his ribs as Broch swings.

memories.memory_text: I told her I led them to the village. I am to blame. The words came out raw, but I couldn't look at her when I said them.
grounded_facts: Eric admits responsibility for leading the Queen's forces to the village. He speaks the line
I led them there. I am to blame.
He avoids eye contact
showing guilt and emotional distress. Snow White responds with empathy
indicating the emotional weight of the moment.

memories.memory_text: I didn't see them coming. One second I was looking at the sun through the trees, the next—fists, boots, masks. Then I was hanging upside down, blood rushing to my head, no weapon, no chance.
grounded_facts: Eric is ambushed while lying on the riverbank

unaware of the approaching dwarves. He is attacked suddenly by multiple figures in battle masks. He is overpowered despite brief resistance. He is captured and hung upside down by a noose around his ankles.

memories.scene_title: 84、EXT. STONE RUINS/ GROVE/ ENCHANTED FOREST - SUNSET.
memories.memory_text: I saw Gort and Nion sing and share rum before they fell to the ground. I chuckled. I said the Queen took their pride when she seized their mines.
grounded_facts: Eric sees Gort and Nion singing and sharing rum before they fall to the ground.
Eric chuckles after seeing them fall.
Eric states that the Queen stole the dwarves' pride when she seized their mines.

memories.scene_title: 87、EXT. ANOTHER PART OF THE ENCHANTED FOREST - DAY.
memories.memory_text: I was tracking the sound of hooves, bow ready, when they stopped dead—I turned, saw Finn’s horse charging past, riderless. That’s when I knew I’d been fooled.
grounded_facts: Eric was running after Finn with bow and arrow at the ready. The sound of galloping hooves suddenly stopped
prompting Eric to stop and become alert. He saw Finn's riderless horse gallop past
which he realized was a distraction. The realization that it was a trick came too late
indicating delayed recognition.

memories.scene_title: 87、EXT. ANOTHER PART OF THE ENCHANTED FOREST - DAY.
memories.memory_text: I barely had time to spin around before Finn came out of the dark and hit me with his sword—just a glancing blow, but it caught me off guard.
grounded_facts: Finn attacked Eric suddenly after the distraction of the horse. Finn
exploded out of the darkness
during a moment of tension and silence. Eric was struck by a glancing blow from Finn's sword. The attack was a surprise
indicating Eric was momentarily vulnerable.

memories.scene_title: 90、EXT. ANOTHER PART OF THE ENCHANTED FOREST - DAY.
memories.memory_text: I heard him say her name—Sara—and that she called for me before he killed her. The sound of it ripped something open inside me.
grounded_facts: Finn says
Your Sara," and claims she called for Eric before her death.", Eric reacts with shock and desperation, asking how Finn knows her name.
This information directly triggers Eric's final attack.

memories.scene_title: 90、EXT. ANOTHER PART OF THE ENCHANTED FOREST - DAY.
memories.memory_text: I drove us both into the spiked branches—felt the wood tear into my back as I slammed Finn into them. It didn't matter. Nothing did.
grounded_facts: Eric lunges at Finn and throws him into a fallen tree with spiked branches.
The branches pierce both Finn and Eric.
Eric initiates the action as a final act of vengeance after Finn's taunt.

memories.memory_text: I saw Finn twitch, his eyes finding mine—he tried to say something, then went still. That’s when I started pulling myself free from the branches tearing into my arms.
grounded_facts: Finn looked at Eric before dying.
Finn's final words were 'I feel...' before he died.
Eric began freeing himself from restraining branches after Finn's death.
The branches were cutting into Eric's flesh.

memories.memory_text: I saw William step forward claiming he’d take Snow White from me, so I told him he’d need a man’s voice to give me orders — then we both drew steel, and I made sure he knew drawing a weapon means nothing unless you’ve got the will to use it.
grounded_facts: Eric sees William introducing himself as Duke Hammond's son.
Eric questions William's presence with the Queen's men.
William states his intent to protect Snow White and asserts authority over her protection.
Eric responds by asserting he is the one who brought her this far.
Eric mocks William's authority by referencing his youth ('wait for your voice to break').
William draws his sword in response.
Eric draws his axe.
Eric delivers the line: 'Anyone can draw steel, boy. It takes a man to use it.'

memories.memory_text: I swung my twin hatchets into the flock of ravens, hacking at the shrieking birds as they swarmed around me, tearing at my face and eyes.
grounded_facts: Eric physically attacks the flock of ravens with his twin hatchets
The ravens are described as shrieking and plucking at his eyes
He acts immediately and aggressively upon seeing the threat
William joins him in fighting the ravens

memories.memory_text: I knelt beside her and saw the poison drain the color from her lips; when the light in her eyes went out, I just stared—I couldn't move, couldn't speak.
grounded_facts: Eric kneels next to Snow White after the ravens vanish
He observes the physical effects of the poison on her
He witnesses the moment of her death
The script describes him as looking dazed afterward

memories.memory_text: I told her the truth about Sara—how I came back from the wars poisoned inside, how my wife saved me even though I wasn't worth saving, and how Snow White brought that part of me back until she was gone too.
grounded_facts: Eric speaks to Snow White's body as if she were alive
He confesses details about his deceased wife Sara
He connects his emotional recovery to Snow White's presence
The confession includes personal guilt and loss

memories.memory_text: I brushed the hair from her face, kissed her lips, and walked away—tossing my flask aside like it meant nothing, like I meant nothing.
grounded_facts: Eric gently touches Snow White's face
He kisses her lips
He discards his flask after leaving the chamber
His action reflects self-disgust

memories.memory_text: I saw the firelights rise from the castle walls and knew they were missiles—no time to explain, I just yanked Snow White's horse aside as the fireball exploded nearby.
grounded_facts: Eric observes small firelights on the castle walls.
Eric recognizes the firelights as incoming fiery missiles.
Eric acts immediately to move Snow White out of danger.
A fireball explodes yards from Snow White after Eric redirects her horse.

memories.memory_text: I straddled Magnus, fed him the poisoned wine, and watched his eyes widen as he choked—his hands weak beneath mine when I told him I would destroy him, just as he destroyed my family.
grounded_facts: Ravenna straddled King Magnus on the bed
She offered him a goblet of poisoned wine
King Magnus drank the wine and began choking
She held his hands down and declared
I will destroy you,
She referenced the destruction of her people and family
Her eyes gleamed in the firelight during the act

memories.memory_text: I drove the dagger into him and left the room. As I passed the guards, I said the king had laid down his sword for the night and walked away.
grounded_facts: Ravenna stabbed King Magnus with an ornate dagger
She emerged from the royal bedroom after the murder
Guards were stationed outside the bedroom
She passed the guards after the killing
She stated the king had laid down his sword for the night
She walked away from the scene with composure

memories.memory_text: I stood at the end of the corridor, the king's blood still on my hands, and watched Snow White's face collapse when she saw her father's body—her fear was sweet, but her heart would be sweeter.
grounded_facts: Ravenna is present in the corridor outside the royal bedroom at night.
The king's lifeless body is visible on the bed inside the chamber.
Snow White sees Ravenna and then the body, realizing the betrayal.
Finn and his knights appear beside Ravenna in support.

memories.memory_text: I ordered them to burn every village that supports the Duke, poison their wells, and put their heads on pikes—let the roads bloom with corpses until he comes out.
grounded_facts: Ravenna commanded scorched-earth tactics against the Duke's supporters
She ordered the poisoning of wells and public executions
She demanded rebels be lured out by extreme violence

memories.memory_text: I saw peasants fighting for the milk from my bath and asked Finn if I wasn't kinder now, remembering when we begged for scraps as children.
grounded_facts: Ravenna saw peasants fighting over bath milk
She recalled begging for scraps with Finn in childhood
She questioned whether she was kinder now

memories.memory_text: I saw the Mirror Man form from the bronze and tell me that Snow White is the cause of my weakening—I felt the truth of it strike like a blade, remembered how I should have crushed her as a child.
grounded_facts: Ravenna sees the Mirror Man emerge from the mirror and deliver the message that Snow White is draining her power
She reacts with shock and staggers physically upon hearing Snow White's name
She speaks aloud her regret for not killing Snow White in childhood

memories.memory_text: When the Mirror Man told me that holding Snow White’s heart would grant me immortality, I laughed—pure, unstoppable joy surged through me, a new destiny unfolding.
grounded_facts: The Mirror Man reveals that consuming Snow White's heart will grant eternal life without further need for youth
Ravenna responds with audible
overjoyed laughter
Finn enters and observes only Ravenna laughing at her reflection
unaware of the mirror entity

memories.memory_text: I struck Finn across the face—he staggered, blood on his skin—and I screamed that he had failed me, that Snow White’s heart was useless to me lost in the dark forest.
grounded_facts: Ravenna physically slapped Finn hard enough to make him stagger
She was enraged about Snow White escaping into the Dark Forest
She explicitly stated that Snow White is no good to her if lost and that she must have her heart
Finn's face was already bleeding before the slap

memories.memory_text: After he challenged me, I touched his face—my finger tracing the wound—and I watched it close beneath my hand, his pain fading as I reminded him that I had given everything to him.
grounded_facts: Finn confronted Ravenna by asking if he hadn't given all to her
Ravenna responded by healing his facial wound with a touch
She said
Have I not given you all? All.
while doing so
His expression became calm after the healing
The moment followed immediately after her violent outburst

memories.memory_text: I saw the pain in his eyes when I mentioned his wife—raw, unguarded grief—and I knew I could twist it. When he grabbed my throat, I didn’t flinch; I welcomed it. His rage was proof he still cared, and that meant he could be used.
grounded_facts: Ravenna observed Eric's emotional reaction when she mentioned his wife.
Eric grabbed Ravenna by the throat in response to her comment about reuniting with his wife.
Ravenna remained calm and did not react to the physical threat, signaling control.
She interpreted Eric's anger and grief as leverage.

memories.memory_text: I leaned in close and told him I could bring his wife back—'A life for a life'—and for a moment, I saw hope flicker in his eyes. He doesn’t believe me, not fully, but he wants to. That want is enough.
grounded_facts: Ravenna offered to resurrect Eric's wife in exchange for retrieving Snow White.
She used the phrase 'A life for a life' to frame the exchange.
Eric showed visible signs of wanting to believe her claim.
Ravenna noted his hesitation and desire, recognizing it as a point of control.

memories.memory_text: I saw the blossom on the dead tree and crushed it to ash—life trying to return where it has no right, just like her. I commanded them to find Snow White; only her heart can restore what was stolen.
grounded_facts: Ravenna is present in the cloister gardens by a dying apple tree with a single blossom.
She looks at the blossom as if it were Snow White.
She crushes the blossom in her hand, reducing it to ash.
She issues the command 'Find her' immediately after.

memories.scene_title: 77、INT.ANTE ROOM - ROYAL PALACE - NIGHT (MOVED FROM B4B).
memories.memory_text: I felt the bare skin on the side of my head and saw that tuft of hair caught on the crown—my own hair—and I called for Finn, my voice tearing through the silence.
grounded_facts: Ravenna is naked and alone in the anteroom.
She notices a bald patch on the side of her head by touch.
She sees a matted tuft of her hair caught on the gilded edge of the crown.
She cries out 'Finn!!!!!!!!!' in a moment of distress.

memories.memory_text: My body seized up without warning—the inkwell tipped, black spreading like a wound across the parchment—and then I was on the floor, writhing, feeling my strength tear away as I aged, helpless despite every command I ever gave.
grounded_facts: Ravenna is sitting at her desk when her body suddenly convulses
The ink bottle spills as she collapses
She falls to the floor and writhes
weakening as her power fades and she begins to age

memories.memory_text: I whispered 'Forgive me, brother' as I severed the bond—knowing he would die, feeling the finality of it, the silence where his life once fed mine, and I did not flinch, though it cost me the last tether to my own blood.
grounded_facts: Ravenna speaks the words 'Forgive me
brother' before cutting the bond
The act of severing the bond directly results in Finn's death
She is aware of the connection between her survival and Finn's life force

memories.scene_title: 95、EXT. STONE RUINS/ GROVE/ ENCHANTED FOREST - DAY.
memories.memory_text: The forest has faded, silent and lifeless, as if my shadow touched it.
grounded_facts: The Enchanted Forest has visibly faded, with no animals or fairies present.
The scene suggests Ravenna's influence is implied through the metaphor of her shadow passing over the forest.

memories.memory_text: I leaned over Snow White as the frost crept across her skin, her breath forming steam in the air, and I told her love always betrays us—she was the only one pure enough to save me, the only one who could break the spell, and I raised the dagger to take her heart.
grounded_facts: Ravenna speaks to Snow White while she is frozen, with frost spreading on her face.
Snow White's breath is visible as steam, indicating she is still alive but immobilized.
Ravenna delivers a monologue about love, betrayal, and the power of 'fairest blood'.
Ravenna draws a sacramental dagger and raises it over Snow White, preparing to extract her heart.

memories.memory_text: I crawled across the floor, my body spent, demanding the mirror tell me where she was—where they took Snow White—but it just stared back with my own hollow face.
grounded_facts: Ravenna enters the mirror room in weakened state after transforming from ravens
She crawls toward the mirror and demands answers about Snow White's location
The mirror does not respond and only reflects her aging
weary appearance

memories.memory_text: When I finally whispered 'help me,' my voice broke, and the Mirror Man mocked me—called my plea pathetic, twisted my face in the glass, and turned away.
grounded_facts: Ravenna shifts from shouting to quietly pleading with the mirror
The Mirror Man responds by mocking her words
He expresses disgust at her desperation and distorts her reflection

memories.memory_text: I heard the Black Knight General report that deserters and rebels were rallying to Snow White, and that the King's standard flies again — the first real threat to my rule.
grounded_facts: Ravenna learns from the Black Knight General that deserters, rebels, and peasants are joining Snow White's cause.
The report includes that the King's standard is flying again, signaling a resurgence of the rightful monarchy.

memories.memory_text: As I turned from the balcony, I commanded that no one harm Snow White — her heart must be taken alive, still warm with blood, no matter what it takes.
grounded_facts: Ravenna explicitly orders that Snow White must not be harmed.
She emphasizes the need to obtain Snow White's heart while it is still warm with blood.

memories.memory_text: I stood on the balcony at dawn and saw Snow White lit by sunlight on the cliffs. I said, 'Come to me.'
grounded_facts: Ravenna is on the balcony of her castle at dawn.
She sees the rebel army's banners on distant cliffs.
She observes Snow White illuminated by a ray of sunlight.
She speaks the command 'Come to me.'

memories.memory_text: I stand on the balcony at dawn, looking at Snow White's distant banner.
grounded_facts: Ravenna is on the balcony of her castle at dawn.
She is looking at Snow White's distant banner.
Her demeanor is confident.

memories.memory_text: I stood on the balcony at dawn, watching my archers take position and the artillery men load the trebuchets—this was no longer a time for waiting.
grounded_facts: Ravenna is present on the balcony during dawn.
She observes archers taking positions.
She observes artillery men loading missiles onto trebuchets.

memories.memory_text: I watched from the balcony as my soldiers fell, their blood staining the ground—I didn’t flinch. Snow White had to come to me alive; the rest were just pieces on the board.
grounded_facts: Ravenna is on the balcony of her castle, observing the battle below.
She shows no concern for the deaths of her soldiers.
She gives a direct command that Snow White must be brought to her alive.
The soldiers' role is to delay Snow White's army, not to win the battle.
She has orchestrated the entire scenario with precise calculation.

memories.memory_text: I turned from the balcony, the wind still carrying the distant clash of battle, and walked into the throne room—let them fight, let them die, it matters not, so long as my prize is delivered.
grounded_facts: Ravenna was on the balcony observing or waiting.
She remained calm and calculated despite her soldiers' situation.
She left the balcony and entered the throne room.

memories.memory_text: I raised my hand and shattered the ceiling into a storm of black shards, watching them slice through Snow White’s allies, pinning some to the floor—her warriors were helpless, trapped in a cage of my making.
grounded_facts: Ravenna used her magic to shatter the black ceiling into millions of shards
The shards attacked Eric
William
and the men
injuring and immobilizing them
The shards formed a barrier that isolated Snow White from her allies

memories.memory_text: I raised my hand and summoned the black shards, forming them into three seven-foot-tall razor-sharp Dark Faeries. I told Snow White to avenge her father.
grounded_facts: Ravenna raised her hand and caused the black shards to fall from the ceiling
She formed the shards into three seven-foot-tall razor-sharp Dark Faeries
She commanded Snow White to avenge her father

memories.memory_text: I twisted Snow White's wrist until her bones cracked and the sword clattered to the ground—she screamed, but I didn't let go. She thought she could fight me, but she was never strong enough.
grounded_facts: Ravenna physically overpowered Snow White
Ravenna twisted Snow White's wrist
Snow White's bones cracked
Snow White dropped her sword
Ravenna maintained control during the confrontation

memories.memory_text: I stepped into the fire and let it roar around me—my skin blistered, then healed. I raised my arms and summoned the black shards to seal the chamber, entombing us in darkness while she watched, broken and bleeding.
grounded_facts: Ravenna stood in the fire unharmed
Ravenna's skin blistered and healed
Ravenna summoned black shards
The black shards covered the walls
The chamber was being sealed off

memories.memory_text: I felt the dagger pierce my chest—Snow White’s hand, so small, so fast—and in that instant, my magic tore apart from within. The spell collapsed, and I knew it was undone.
grounded_facts: Ravenna was stabbed in the chest by Snow White during the ritual.
She gasped and locked eyes with Snow White immediately after being stabbed.
The incantation 'By fairest blood it is done' was interrupted, and the ritual failed.

memories.memory_text: I pulled the blade out and felt my face wither—skin cracking, breath failing—as I fell beneath the mirror. I reached up, searching its surface for salvation, but it showed me nothing. No power. No life. Only death.
grounded_facts: After pulling out the dagger, Ravenna rapidly aged and collapsed to the floor below the mirror.
She looked at the mirror and reached toward it in her final moments.
The mirror did not respond or offer any aid.

memories.memory_text: I felt her hand on my chest, warm and steady, and in that moment I reached up and touched her face—like a mother might—whispering, 'See child, we are bound,' as if some truth had finally surfaced through all the blood and rage.
grounded_facts: Snow White placed her hand on Ravenna's chest.
Ravenna placed her wrinkled hand on Snow White's face in a motherly gesture.
Ravenna whispered 'See child, we are bound' to Snow White.

memories.memory_text: I looked up into the light haloing her face, the clouds shifting above, and as it spilled over me, I felt the warmth fade—my breath slowed, the world dimmed, and the light in my eyes went out.
grounded_facts: Ravenna stared at Snow White's face illuminated by a shaft of light from the ceiling.
Clouds moved across the disc of light above, causing the illumination to shift.
The light faded from Ravenna's eyes as she died.

memories.memory_text: I see a halo of light around her head—Snow White—just before the world goes dark.
grounded_facts: Ravenna's eyes are open at the moment of death.
Her final visual perception is of a halo of light surrounding Snow White's head.

memories.memory_text: I remember warmth and light, a soft voice singing as hands passed me from my mother’s arms to my father’s; then the cool glint of a mirror near his face as my cries filled the room.
grounded_facts: Snow White was born in a royal bedroom attended by midwives and physicians.
She was held by her mother first, then given to King Magnus.
Her cries grew louder when the King held her.
The King turned toward a mirror while holding her.

memories.memory_text: I ran as fast as I could, the injured bird cradled in my hands, feeling its faint heartbeat against my palms, shouting for William to keep up as the castle came into view.
grounded_facts: Snow White is running with a wounded bird in her hands.
She is calling out to William, urging him to hurry.
They are moving from a seaside village toward a castle, passing dunes and hearing waves.
The scene takes place during the day with sunlight on the sand and surroundings.

memories.memory_text: I ran into my parents’ room, breathless, holding the injured magpie we found in the woods—I remember how its wing trembled in my hands.
grounded_facts: Snow White entered her parents' chamber with the magpie.
She stated, 'We found it in the woods.'
She physically held the injured bird before giving it to the Queen.

memories.memory_text: My mother touched my heart and said I have a rare beauty inside that I must hold dear. She said I would need its strength to become Queen one day. I didn’t understand why she looked sad, or why my father was hiding his sorrow.
grounded_facts: The Queen touched Snow White's heart while speaking.
The Queen said Snow White has a rare inner beauty that she must hold dear.
The Queen said Snow White would need the strength of that beauty to become Queen.
Snow White noticed her parents were sad, though she did not fully understand why.

memories.memory_text: A single tear rolled down my cheek as I walked through the snow-covered garden, my father's arm around me, the silence heavy after my mother's passing.
grounded_facts: Snow White is in the cloister garden with her father, King Magnus, after her mother's death.
The garden is covered in snow and lacks the red rose previously present.
A tear rolls down Snow White's cheek, indicating her grief.
Mourners and onlookers are present, observing her reaction.

memories.memory_text: I stood behind Ravenna, holding the heavy silk of her dress, and looked up at her with what must have seemed like adoration—though my heart was anything but certain.
grounded_facts: Snow White is positioned behind Ravenna during the wedding procession.
She is physically holding the folds of Ravenna's wedding dress.
She looks up at Ravenna, described as 'adoringly' in the scene.

memories.memory_text: I was asleep when the bell started ringing—sharp and sudden—and at first I thought it was part of a dream, but then I heard people shouting, voices full of panic, and I sat up, heart pounding, knowing something was wrong.
grounded_facts: Snow White was asleep when she first heard the bell.
She initially believed the sound might be part of a dream.
She heard cries of alarm following the bell.
She sat up in bed and then got up in response.

memories.memory_text: I ran to my father’s room, saw Ravenna standing there, and for a moment felt relief—until I saw my father’s body on the bed, lifeless, and realized she had done it.
grounded_facts: Snow White rushed toward her father's chamber at night.
She initially felt relieved upon seeing Ravenna.
She noticed her father's lifeless body on the bed.
She realized Ravenna was responsible for his death.
Tears of shock welled in her eyes before she fled.

memories.memory_text: I woke in the dark and saw her standing there again, watching me—silent as always—but this time, I couldn't stay quiet anymore. I asked her, 'Why?' and watched her turn away without a word.
grounded_facts: Snow White was in a darkened cell in the north tower.
Ravenna stood outside the cell, watching her without speaking.
Snow White opened her eyes and made eye contact with Ravenna.
Snow White broke the silence by asking 'Why?'
Ravenna did not respond and walked away.

memories.scene_title: 24、INT. PRISON TOWER/ SNOW WHITE'S CELL - DAY.
memories.memory_text: I heard footsteps approaching, so I quickly got into bed and pretended to sleep until the guard passed; once he was gone, I went to the door and called out, 'Hello?... What's my name?' to the woman in the opposite cell.
grounded_facts: Snow White hears approaching footsteps and pretends to sleep.
After the guard leaves, Snow White approaches her cell door and speaks to Rose.
Snow White initiates contact by asking a question and then smiles upon hearing Rose's response.

memories.scene_title: 26、INT. PRISON TOWER/ SNOW WHITE'S CELL - DAY.
memories.memory_text: I heard Rose say she was trying to reach Duke Hammond's castle, and I felt a sudden rush of hope—he might still be alive, still fighting for my father's name.
grounded_facts: Rose mentioned trying to reach Duke Hammond's castle while fleeing.
Snow White reacted with visible hope upon hearing the Duke's name.
Snow White asked if the Duke was alive and still fighting in her father's name.

memories.scene_title: 26、INT. PRISON TOWER/ SNOW WHITE'S CELL - DAY.
memories.memory_text: I couldn't wait to ask—my voice broke as I demanded to know if William had survived; Rose didn't answer, and that silence terrified me.
grounded_facts: Snow White urgently asked Rose about William's fate.
Snow White referred to William as 'the Duke's son'.
Rose responded that she did not know, shaking her head.

memories.scene_title: 30、INT. PRISON TOWER/ SNOW WHITE'S CELL - DAY.
memories.memory_text: I saw the magpies flapping at the roof's edge, drawn to something—a loose roofing nail—and I reached through the bars until I could wiggle it free, yanking it hard just before footsteps echoed in the hall.
grounded_facts: Two magpies appeared in the window and moved toward the edge of the roof.
Snow White noticed a roofing nail at the spot where the magpies hovered.
She reached out, loosened the nail by moving it back and forth, and pulled it free just before hearing footsteps.

memories.scene_title: 30、INT. PRISON TOWER/ SNOW WHITE'S CELL - DAY.
memories.memory_text: I kept still, gripping the nail in my fist as Finn touched my cheek and moved his hand toward my chest, and when he leaned in—dagger in his other hand—I slashed him across the face, feeling the sharp resistance as blood sprayed.
grounded_facts: Finn entered the cell, touched Snow White’s arm, hair, cheek, and moved toward her chest.
Snow White held the roofing nail tightly in her closed hand during the encounter.
As Finn leaned closer and reached for his dagger, she used the nail to slash his face, causing blood to fly.

memories.memory_text: I rushed down the steps to Greta’s cell, heart pounding, only to freeze at the sight of Rose—her face lined with age, her hair gray, barely recognizable.
grounded_facts: Snow White arrives at Greta's cell after hurrying down the steps.
She is shocked by Rose's appearance.
Rose is now an old woman, which is unexpected and disturbing to Snow White.

memories.memory_text: I took her hands, saw the fear and urgency in her eyes, then dropped the keys and ran when Finn shouted and the guards were called.
grounded_facts: Snow White takes Rose's hands and looks into her eyes.
Rose silently urges her to leave.
Finn appears at the cell door, yelling 'Guards!'
Snow White throws the keys into an empty cell and flees.

memories.memory_text: I burst into the open air, gasping at the sky and the light—so much space after the stone walls—and then I ran, following the magpies as they darted ahead, leading me to a narrow opening at the base of the wall.
grounded_facts: Snow White exits the castle into the courtyard during the day.
She reacts strongly to the sensory experience of fresh air and open sky.
Magpies appear suddenly, fly toward her, then away, guiding her movement.
She runs toward the portcullis and then changes direction to follow the birds.
She slides on her hip and enters a small opening that leads into the sewers.
Guards pursue but cannot fit through the opening.

memories.memory_text: I wade through thick, foul-smelling muck, each step heavy and repulsive, but I keep moving forward.
grounded_facts: Snow White is walking through deep, stinking sludge in a sewer.
She is physically immersed in a challenging and unpleasant environment.

memories.memory_text: I rode into the village and could barely recognize it—fires had blackened the homes, and the people, even the children, were little more than bones beneath hollow eyes. When they came toward me, I saw not hope but hunger in their faces, and I turned my horse and fled as the Queen’s men appeared behind them.
grounded_facts: The village is largely destroyed, with most cottages razed or boarded up.
Snow White sees a group of starving villagers, including children, approaching her.
She reacts with emotional distress, shedding tears as she observes the devastation.
The villagers' initial approach shifts in her perception to one of dangerous desperation.
She rides away quickly, looking back to see the Queen's men chasing her.

memories.memory_text: I crested the hill and saw the Dark Forest rise before me like a wall of ancient trees, mist curling from it like something alive, and for a moment I couldn't move — then I spotted the riders gaining on me and drove my mare forward.
grounded_facts: Snow White reaches the top of a hill and sees the Dark Forest for the first time.
The forest is described as a vast, intimidating presence with mist that evokes a sense of evil.
She hesitates upon seeing it, indicating internal conflict or fear.
She notices riders approaching, which prompts her to continue forward despite her fear.

memories.scene_title: 42、EXT. MISTY BOGS/ ENTRANCE TO THE DARK FOREST - DAY.
memories.memory_text: I fell hard onto the ground when the mare slipped in the bog, and I reached for her reins, but she pulled away—afraid to risk me—leaving me alone as the riders' shadows appeared through the fog.
grounded_facts: Snow White was thrown from the mare when it lost footing in the misty bog.
She attempted to grab the reins but the horse pulled away to avoid endangering her.
She saw the silhouettes of the riders reappearing in the distance before running into the forest.

memories.memory_text: I remember running through the fog, the trees twisting into hooded figures, and something cold brushing my face like a hand—then everything went black.
grounded_facts: Snow White tripped over a root and landed in a patch of large mushrooms that released clouds of pollen.
She began experiencing vivid hallucinations, including anthropomorphized trees, eyeless owls, black oil from trunks, and a vine that seemed to touch her.
She stumbled repeatedly, saw vampire bats and skeletal forms emerging from trees, and eventually collapsed unconscious.

memories.memory_text: Strong hands grabbed my ankles and yanked me out of the bushes—I kicked and twisted, but he pinned me down with his boot, cold and unrelenting, and when I saw his face, I knew I was caught.
grounded_facts: Snow White is ambushed from hiding when the huntsman grabs her by the feet
She struggles violently but is overpowered and pinned by the huntsman
The huntsman uses physical force to subdue her and lifts her up after the struggle

memories.memory_text: When Finn appeared, I tried to warn the huntsman—he wouldn't listen, then put the knife to my throat to silence me, and I felt the cold edge press just enough to make me stop breathing.
grounded_facts: Finn and his men arrive while Snow White is restrained
Snow White warns Eric that Finn cannot be trusted
Eric silences her by placing a knife at her throat

memories.memory_text: I remember the little girl Lily showing me her straw doll, marking its face with a scar just like hers, and handing it to me—when I said 'She's beautiful,' Lily smiled and ran off, and something in me cracked.
grounded_facts: Lily made a straw doll and marked it with a scar.
Snow White said 'She's beautiful' in response to the doll.
Lily smiled and ran away after the exchange.

memories.memory_text: I told Eric I'd never spoken the words out loud before—that I was the king's daughter—because saying it made it real, and I wasn't sure I believed it myself, not after years alone in the dark.
grounded_facts: Snow White confessed to Eric that she had never said she was the king's daughter aloud.
She associated the confession with disbelief and emotional weight.
She recalled memories of her father during the conversation.

memories.memory_text: I woke up and Eric was gone—then I caught the smell of smoke, stepped outside, and looked up to see the sky lit with burning arrows raining down on the village.
grounded_facts: Snow White awakens in Anna's hut and notices Eric is not there.
She detects the smell of smoke before going outside.
She sees burning arrows falling from the sky, setting the village on fire.

memories.memory_text: I stood at the top of the stairs in my white funeral robes, heart pounding, as everyone below looked up at me. I began to walk down and told the Duke I was ready to ride by his side. I told him I could vanquish the Queen.
grounded_facts: Snow White appears at the top of the stairs in white funeral robes.
She feels her heart pounding as she descends the stairs.
The crowd is silent and riveted, reacting to her as if she is a miracle.
Snow White tells the Duke she has rested long enough and is ready to ride by his side in battle.
She declares she can vanquish the Queen and that she is the only one who can.

memories.memory_text: When Duke Hammond said I should stay behind the walls, I told him I had rested long enough—and that I am the only one who can defeat Ravenna, my voice steady even as my heart raced.
grounded_facts: Duke Hammond suggests Snow White remain safe behind walls.
Snow White responds that she has rested long enough.
She declares she can vanquish Ravenna and is the only one who can defeat her.
Her voice is gentle but full of conviction.

memories.memory_text: I raised my shield against the storm of arrows, the impact stinging my arms, and shouted over the chaos—I had promised to stand with them, and I wouldn't break that vow.
grounded_facts: Snow White is on the beach at dawn, under arrow fire from castle archers.
She uses her shield to protect herself from incoming arrows.
William urges her to retreat, but she refuses, citing her promise to stand with the allied forces.

memories.memory_text: I stormed into the throne room, heart pounding, and shouted at her — 'It ends today... I have come for YOU!' — my voice echoing off the cold stone, the weight of ten years behind those words.
grounded_facts: Snow White enters the throne room with heavy breathing, showing fear and determination.
She speaks the line 'It ends today... I have come for YOU!' directly to Ravenna.
She references being locked away for ten years, implying deep personal history.

memories.memory_text: I turned just in time to see the ceiling explode — black shards raining down, slicing through William, Eric, and the others — their screams cut short as they were pinned to the floor, and I could do nothing.
grounded_facts: Ravenna raises her hand and the black ceiling shatters into millions of shards.
Snow White turns to see Eric, William, and the men caught in the attack.
The shards act like razor blades, skewering some men to the stone floor.
Snow White witnesses the massacre without being physically harmed in that moment.
